$generated_by: generate_vault_schema.py -- do not hand-edit, regenerate instead
note: Every collection's ontology id is a plain stable string by default (the collection name itself) -- no Ontology-service registration required for store/fetch to work. See vault.schema_ids in a program's config/settings.json to map a collection to a registered Ontology W3ID instead, which is only needed for cross-platform interop.

field_authority.note: Which source READERS should prefer when multiple collections report the same quantity. Walk each list in order and take the first collection that has a value for the animal/date at hand. Writers never merge or overwrite: every source keeps its own records, provenance stays intact, and promoting a better source later (e.g. CRV) is an edit to this table -- no re-upload, no data loss.

lactation_days.note: CRV registration -- leading as soon as it is uploaded
robot's own registration; less reliable, fallback only

lactation_number.note: CRV registration -- leading as soon as it is uploaded
robot's own registration; less reliable, fallback only

milking_speed.note: the robot measures this itself -- authoritative, CRV has no equivalent

read_by: chatbot (ad-hoc questions over any collection, schema-driven)

schema_version.description: Bumped when this record's shape changes.

id.description: Unique within the collection; used for dedup on upload.

animal_number.description: 4-digit animal tag number (records with any other length are dropped).

registration_number.description: Official (KI/NRS-style) registration number of the animal.

timestamp.description: Milking timestamp, ISO 8601, farm-local time (no timezone offset stored).

status.description: Milking outcome, normalized from the robot's endOfMilkingStatus.

enum.!: milking not fully completed / robot-reported issue
enum.#: unknown or other status (fallback for anything unrecognized)

yield_raw.description: Yield exactly as the robot reported it — NOT liters. Convert at display time: liters = yield_raw / yield_divisor (yield_divisor defaults to 1000, see dashboard settings).

source.description: Constant identifying which DataSource produced this record.

how_to_query.graphql: metaEnvelopes(filter: { ontologyId: "milking_controle_data" }, first: 100, after: $cursor) { edges { node { parsed } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

read_by: dashboard (/api/stats: feed x milk join, efficiency, leftovers)
chatbot (ad-hoc questions over any collection, schema-driven)

schema_version.description: Bumped when this record's shape changes.

id.description: Unique within the collection; used for dedup on upload.

animal_number.description: 4-digit animal tag number (same numbering as milking_controle_data).

timestamp.description: Start of the milking visit this feeding belongs to (ISO 8601, farm-local). Join key to milking_controle_data records of the same animal and visit.

all_feed_consumed.description: Whether the cow finished the dispensed feed (robot's Ja/Nee column); null when the export holds anything else.

feed_a_raw.description: Amount of feed type A exactly as the robot reported it (robot-configured unit, typically grams). Interpretation is the reader's job, like yield_raw.

feed_b_raw.description: Amount of feed type B, as reported.

feed_c_raw.description: Amount of feed type C, as reported.

feed_d_raw.description: Amount of feed type D, as reported.

source.description: Constant identifying which DataSource produced this record.

how_to_query.graphql: metaEnvelopes(filter: { ontologyId: "feed_distribution_data" }, first: 100, after: $cursor) { edges { node { parsed } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

read_by: chatbot (ad-hoc questions over any collection, schema-driven)

schema_version.description: Bumped when this record's shape changes.

id.description: Unique within the collection (one snapshot per cow per report date); used for dedup on upload.

animal_number.description: 4-digit animal tag number (same numbering as milking_controle_data).

report_date.description: Date the snapshot describes (ISO 8601 date), taken from the file name.

milk_24h_kg.description: Milk produced in the last 24 hours (kg), as reported by the robot.

milk_10d_avg_kg.description: 10-day average of the 24h production (kg), as computed by the robot.

lactation_number.description: Lactation number according to the ROBOT's herd registration. Less reliable than CRV; see field_authority in VAULT_SCHEMA.json -- CRV (mpr_uitslag) becomes the leading source for this once uploaded.

average_milking_speed_kg_min.description: Average milking speed (kg/min), as measured by the robot. The robot is the authoritative source for this field.

lactation_days.description: Days in lactation according to the ROBOT's registration. Less reliable than CRV; see field_authority in VAULT_SCHEMA.json.

source.description: Constant identifying which DataSource produced this record.

how_to_query.graphql: metaEnvelopes(filter: { ontologyId: "milking_production_data" }, first: 100, after: $cursor) { edges { node { parsed } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

read_by: chatbot (ad-hoc questions over any collection, schema-driven)

schema_version.description: Bumped when this record's shape changes.

id.description: Unique within the collection, and deterministic for a given analysis date + analysed dataset + finding + subject. Re-running on unchanged data therefore stores nothing (the eVault has no overwrite-on-id), while re-running after new data was uploaded produces a fresh batch.

created_at.description: When the agent produced this insight (ISO 8601).

enum.herd_yield_change: herd-level yield moved vs the baseline window
enum.herd_failure_rate: share of milkings not finishing normally is high
enum.cow_yield_drop: one cow produces clearly less than her own baseline
enum.cow_yield_rise: one cow produces clearly more than her own baseline
enum.cow_interval_rise: one cow visits the robot noticeably less often
enum.cow_feed_left: one cow repeatedly leaves feed uneaten (early illness signal)
enum.herd_feed_efficiency_change: liters of milk per kg of feed moved vs baseline
enum.cow_speed_drop: one cow's milking speed dropped between production reports
enum.cow_multi_signal: several independent analyses flagged the SAME cow in the same period -- the pattern most easily missed when scanning a list of single-quantity findings
enum.cow_recovered: one cow dipped clearly below her own baseline and is back at her level -- positive: closes the loop on an earlier drop

scope.description: What the insight is about: {'animal_number': 5337} for one cow, {'herd': true} for the whole herd. Use animal_number to join back to milking_controle_data.

period.description: The measurement dates this insight is based on. The recent window runs data_from -> data_until, compared against the baseline window baseline_from -> data_from. Anchored on the newest record in the vault, NOT on the analysis date -- if data_until is well before created_at, the underlying data is running behind.

title.description: One-line summary, written by the model.
title.example: Cow 5337 is producing 18% below her own average

body.description: Explanation and suggested action, written by the model.
body.example: Her daily yield fell from 34.8 to 28.5 L/day over the past week...

evidence.description: The exact figures this insight is based on, computed in Python (never by the model). Makes every insight auditable.

how_to_query.graphql: metaEnvelopes(filter: { ontologyId: "milking_insights" }, first: 100, after: $cursor) { edges { node { parsed } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

milking_insights.note: Insights are written by a separate post-platform. 'evidence' holds figures computed in Python; 'title'/'body' are worded by the model named in 'model' -- so every insight can be checked against its own numbers.

mpr_uitslag.note: CRV MPR results per cow per sampling (Dieroverzicht): levensnummer + werknummer, cell count, fat/protein %, urea, lactation number and days. Parser not built yet. Once uploaded, this is the leading source for lactation data (see field_authority) and the only source for cell count. Links to the robot collections via werknummer (animal_number); the levensnummer <-> werknummer mapping lives in milking_controle_data (registration_number).
